note_data.title: Software
keyPoints: Binary system uses base 2 with 0s and 1s to process data in logic gates/transistors, store data in registers, and process data on computers

Hexadecimal uses base 16 system with digits 0-9 and alphabets A-F, used in colour codes, error codes and IP & MAC addresses because it is easily understandable, easier to debug, and takes less space on the screen
Each shift left is equivalent to multiplying the binary number by 2^n (where n is the number of places shifted)
Each shift right is equivalent to dividing the binary number by 2^n (where n is the number of places shifted)
Two's complement format is used to represent negative binary numbers where the leftmost bit is changed to a negative value and determines the sign of the number

Overflow error occurs when the value is greater than the maximum value of the register - it is the result of carrying out a calculation that produces a value too large for the computer's allocated word size
A character set is a list of all characters and symbols represented by a computer where each character and symbol has a unique value
Sampling is used by determining the amplitude of the sound after set intervals to give an approximate representation of the sound wave
The accuracy of a sound file increases with greater sample rates and sampling resolutions, but so does the file size

definitions.definition: A list of all characters and symbols represented by a computer where each character and symbol has a unique value
Used by determining the amplitude of the sound after set intervals. This gives an approximate representation of the sound wave
The number of samples taken per second
The number of bits required per sample (i.e: bit depth)
The result of carrying out a calculation that produces a value too large for the computer's allocated word size

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give characteristics or features with some detail
Show similarities AND differences between two items

modelAnswer.question: Explain the difference between a compiler and an interpreter. [4 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: A compiler translates the entire high-level language program into machine code before execution, whereas an interpreter translates and executes the program line by line during runtime. A compiler produces an executable file that can run independently, while an interpreter requires the original source code to be present each time the program runs. Programs compiled by a compiler run faster because translation has already occurred, but interpreter-based programs are easier to debug because errors are identified line by line during execution.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Compiler translates entire program before execution
Mark 2: Interpreter translates line by line during runtime
Mark 3: Compiler produces executable file/interpreter needs source code
Mark 4: Speed difference or debugging difference explained

commonMistakes: Losing leftmost 1 bits while shifting left will cause an error because the limit of maximum number of left shifts possible will have been exceeded
Losing rightmost 1 bits while shifting right will cause an error because the limit of maximum number of right shifts possible will have been exceeded
Not recognizing when overflow error occurs - when the extra carry in the answer indicates that overflow error has occurred and the answer is greater than the maximum value the register can store
examinerTips: When converting binary to hexadecimal, break into sets of 4 bits from right to left
For two's complement conversion of negative numbers: write the positive number in binary, invert all the values, then add 1 to the inverted binary value
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Software classification diagram showing System Software (Operating System, Utility Software, Device Drivers) and Application Software (General Purpose, Special Purpose) with examples under each category
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (3 marks) — they asked students to describe what is meant by open-source software, focusing on access to source code and modification rights
This topic was tested in O/N 2022 P1 Q12 (2 marks) — students had to give two examples of application software from a list of options
This topic was tested in M/J 2024 P2 Q6 (4 marks) — they asked students to describe the differences between proprietary and open-source software with specific examples
This topic was tested in O/N 2023 P1 Q8 (1 mark) — students had to identify utility software from multiple choice options including antivirus, word processor, and web browser
This topic was tested in M/J 2022 P2 Q5 (3 marks) — they asked about firewalls being hardware or software-based with justification required

workedExample.question: Describe the differences between proprietary and open-source software. Give one example of each type. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Define proprietary software - focus on source code access restrictions
Step 2: Define open-source software - emphasize user freedoms with source code
Step 3: Compare key differences - modification rights, cost, support, redistribution
Step 4: Provide clear example of proprietary software with brief explanation
Step 5: Provide clear example of open-source software with brief explanation
workedExample.answer: Proprietary software: User cannot access the source code, cannot modify or fix bugs, usually costs money to purchase, support comes from the company. Open-source software: User can access the source code, can modify and redistribute it, usually free, community-based support. Example of proprietary: Microsoft Windows - users cannot modify the operating system code. Example of open-source: Linux - users can access, modify and redistribute the source code freely.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark each for defining proprietary and open-source, 2 marks for explaining key differences, 1 mark each for appropriate examples with brief justification

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused system software with application software' — always clearly distinguish that system software manages hardware/computer operations while application software performs user tasks
Examiner Report 2024: 'Students often gave vague examples of software types' — always provide specific, recognizable examples like 'Microsoft Word' rather than just 'word processor'

Examiner Report 2022: 'Candidates frequently missed key features when describing open-source software' — always mention source code access, modification rights, and redistribution when explaining open-source software
note_data.keyDiagram: Software Classification Hierarchy: Draw a tree diagram with 'Software' at the top, branching into 'System Software' and 'Application Software'. Under System Software: Operating Systems, Device Drivers, Utilities (with examples like antivirus, disk cleanup). Under Application Software: General Purpose (word processors, spreadsheets, databases) and Special Purpose (payroll systems, library systems). Include a separate box showing 'Software Licensing' with Proprietary vs Open-source branches, listing key characteristics and examples for each.
note_data.quickRecap: Data representation involves converting between binary, denary and hexadecimal number systems. Binary uses base 2 for computer processing, while hexadecimal uses base 16 for easier human readability in applications like error codes and addresses. Two's complement represents negative numbers, and logical shifts multiply or divide by powers of 2. Text, sound and images are all converted to binary using character sets, sampling, and pixel representation respectively.
